fields.description:
## Why
The declarative descriptors + the generic chunker/router are the engine of the gate; production prompts live
in the workflow-engine prompt library (not the experiment JSON).

## What
- criteria_v8 descriptors -> production prompt library (resolve_prompt / prompts-as-files).
- Generic orchestrator: applies_at level/type routing; facet chunker (base_chunk x size_factor); exec-tier
  dispatch (single-turn / 2-step / agent); overlay routing (deterministic + LLM-router).

## Scope
- NO SILENT TRUNCATION of the reviewed artifact. The gate must review the FULL plan + children as they
  exist in rebar (read the compiled `rebar show` description, not a capped/event view) — truncating the
  reviewed content invalidates the review. The orchestrator reads full bodies; arbitrary char caps are a bug.
- CONTENT-BUDGET handling when the full content would exceed the model context (distinct from rubric-chunking
  and the LLM-call budget): in priority order — (1) feed whole (normal tickets fit a large context window);
  (2) STRUCTURAL DECOMPOSITION — review each child/ticket at its own level + the epic's own ACs and aggregate
  (the proportionate-scrutiny model; no single oversized context); (3) SECTION-CHUNK a single giant ticket by
  AC/section with FULL coverage (never drop the tail); (4) if even that can't cover it, return INDETERMINATE
  for the uncovered part — never silently certify a partial review. Supporting context that is genuinely huge
  (e.g. the ISF linked session log) uses DETERMINISTIC pre-retrieval of decision-bearing sections with the
  selection RECORDED as coverage — lossy but explicit, and only for supporting context, never the primary plan.
- Coverage is RECORDED on the attestation so a partial/over-budget review is visible, not hidden.
- Built ON the workflow engine; the exact SHAPE (gate-as-workflow vs bespoke; dynamic fan-out handling)
  depends on the `6f2d` (soupy-spool-revel) brainstorm this epic depends_on. Reuse rebar.llm extension points.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registry loads from the prompt library; the coverage guard passes.
- [ ] Orchestrator filters via applies_at, packs by facet, dispatches by exec tier.
- [ ] No criterion greps the codebase except the code-grounding set (E4/G1G2/A1/G6).
- [ ] The orchestrator reviews the FULL plan + children (no truncation of the reviewed artifact); over-budget content is handled by structural decomposition / section-chunk-with-full-coverage / INDETERMINATE — never a silent truncation — with coverage recorded on the attestation.
